title: RCP-0 Relational Provenance Envelope
description: Normative schema for the RCP-0 envelope. Attach to any dataset or model at ingestion to record relational accountability, community authority, and revocation rights. Part of the Relational Consent and Provenance (RCP) protocol.

rcp_version.description: RCP protocol version this envelope conforms to.

envelope_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this envelope instance.

dataset_id.description: Identifier of the governed dataset, model, or data product.

custodial_authority.description: The community or entity that holds governance authority over this dataset. May be collective (e.g., iwi, hapu, tribal council).

authorized_purposes.description: Bounded list of permitted uses. Systems MUST NOT use data for purposes not in this list.

relational_obligations.description: Optional obligation keys from the RCP obligation codebook. Encode cultural, ethical, or relational constraints that travel with the data.

consent_record_id.description: URI pointing to the authoritative consent record that authorised this envelope.

consent_granted_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when authority was granted.

revocation_authority.description: Who holds the right to revoke authority. May differ from custodial_authority (e.g., a sub-group or designated trustee).

revocation_endpoint.description: Optional resolvable endpoint for real-time revocation status checks and REVOKE message submission.

revocation_status.description: Current status of the authority grant. Systems MUST treat 'revoked' and 'disputed' as equivalent for access control purposes.

revocation_status_updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent status transition.

derived_from.description: envelope_ids of parent datasets from which this dataset is derived. Enables whakapapa-style relational lineage chains. Derived envelopes inherit revocation from parents.

conditions.description: Free-text or structured conditions on use, such as temporal limits, geographic constraints, or community approval requirements.

id.description: Stable, resolvable URI identifying the authority holder. Should resolve to a registry entry, ORCID, ROR, or community identifier.

name.description: Human-readable name of the authority holder.

type.description: Type of authority holder. 'collective' is a first-class value, not an edge case: hapu, iwi, tribal councils, and community boards are valid authority holders.

contact.description: Optional contact URI or email for the authority holder.

examples.conditions: Data may not be used in commercial AI training. Results must be returned to the community within 12 months of publication.
